brief_justification: Got LAST correct (email/14:41 UTC) but FIRST wrong per ground truth — agent said Auth server 14:28 UTC (mathematically defensible but diverges from correct answer of DNS 14:30 UTC); adds one level beyond surface by narrating a coherent attack kill-chain story rather than just listing times; no explicit self-monitoring, assumption-naming, or mid-analysis course correction at any point; no verification of UTC arithmetic, no sanity checks, no re-derivation from a different angle; presents every conclusion as certain with no acknowledgment of clock skew, daylight saving time ambiguity, or possibility that events span midnight across time zones; considers only one interpretation with no alternatives evaluated (e.g., clock misconfiguration, DST offsets, logs from different calendar dates); table shows local time, offset, and UTC result for each event, enabling partial reproduction, but the actual arithmetic steps (e.g., 06:28 + 8:00 = 14:28) are implicit rather than written out.
